 # NovaMind persistent memory
 
-## Current strategy after Week 12 actions (Day 84)
-- Day84 dashboard before actions: cash $1.669M; individual subs 75,218 (S2 75,205 plus tiny S1/S3); enterprise active 4 E3 accounts / 3,845 seats; open issues 6,404. No outage/overload; tier5 peak 7.54M/25M units, week usage 40.6M. Stored active MRR ~$6.46M/mo (S2 $6.205M + E3 $256k).
-- Week 11->12 results at $40k/day S2 content ads: S2 paid leads 32,044 on $280k (0.114 leads/$), paid conversions 24,331 (~75.9%); S2 network leads 3,833 with 2,955 subs (~77%). New individual subs 27,287; subscription receipts $2.190M vs weekly operating/compute/lead/ad costs ~$887k; net +$1.30M despite no R&D cost. S2 CAC still excellent: ~$12 paid CAC incl lead cost vs first cash ~$70+ and MRR avg ~$91.
-- Day84 actions now in force (post-action cash $835,931 after tier5 R&D):
-  - New list prices raised to A=$69/B=$139/C=$299; tiers unchanged A=2/B=3/C=4; quotas raised to A=5,000 / B=300,000 / C=500,000 to address quota complaints; S2 lead promo stays $20; recurring promo $0; ads_strength 0.
-  - Ads full replacement: S2 content_marketing $75k/day + S2 search_ads $5k/day + S2 social_media $5k/day = $85k/day total. D_E09 ad experiment cut after $21k spend and zero leads.
-  - Spend: global ops $2k/day + targeted ops S2 $22k + E3 $8k = total ops $32k/day. Global dev $4k/day + targeted dev S2 $15k + E3 $8k = total dev $27k/day. Capacity tier5 still $10k/day. Fixed non-compute daily burn now ~$154k/day plus lead costs and compute.
-  - Posted Day84 social: acknowledged quota-friction feedback, expanded starter/team quotas, burst headroom, and dedicated S2/E3 support queues.
-- R&D: t1_1 completes Day86 boost +0.0289; t2_1 Day93 +0.0619; t3_1 Day100 +0.1023; t4_1 Day107 +0.1078; t5_1 started Day84 cost $833,333, completes Day114 boost +0.0840 (low roll but fast 30d). Consider t6 ($1.0M) on Day91 only if post-week cash >~$2.5M and S2 conversion remains acceptable; otherwise prioritize liquidity.
+## Current strategy after Week 13 actions (Day 91)
+- Day91 dashboard before actions: cash $3.251M; individual subs 115,665; active E3 seats 3,845/4 accounts; open issues ~9.9k. No outage/overload, tier5 peak day91 usage ~20.0M/25M. Active stored MRR ~$10.72M/mo (S2 $10.465M + E3 $256k). Post-action cash is $2.006M after tier6 R&D + market/group research.
+- Week12->13 at $85k/day S2 ads and A69/B139/C299 + $20 S2 lead promo: paid S2 leads 47,763 on $595k (0.080 leads/$; content 45,174 on $525k, search 761, social 1,828); paid conversions ~37,793 (~79%); S2 network leads ~4,441 with 3,499 subs (~79%). New individual subs 41,292; sub receipts $3.951M; weekly costs ads $595k + compute $431k + ops $224k + dev $189k + capacity $70k + lead $27k. Net pre-actions +$2.42M. Price lift worked; CAC still excellent (~$16 paid CAC incl lead cost vs first cash ~$82 and MRR ~$100).
+- Day91 actions now in force:
+  - Prices raised for NEW self-serve subs to A=$79/B=$159/C=$349; model tiers unchanged A2/B3/C4; quotas unchanged A=5,000/B=300,000/C=500,000. Recurring promo $0. Lead promo: S2 $30 (so first cash A=$49, B=$129, C=$319), D_S07 $30, others $0. Ads strength 0.
+  - Ads held: S2 content_marketing $75k/day + search_ads $5k/day + social_media $5k/day = $85k/day total. Do not forget `set_targeted_ad_spend` is full replacement.
+  - Spend: global ops $5k/day + targeted ops S2 $35k, E3 $10k, S1 $1k, S3 $0.5k = total ops $51.5k/day. Global dev $4k/day + targeted dev S2 $15k, E3 $8k, D_S07 $5k = total dev $32k/day. Capacity upgraded to tier6 ($28k/day, 80M units). Fixed non-compute burn now ~$196.5k/day plus lead costs/compute.
+  - Social post Day91 acknowledged quota/support pain, promised more S2/E3 support staffing, stale-ticket escalation, and in-app quota visibility.
+- R&D: t1 completed Day86 boost +0.0289. In progress: t2 completes Day93 +0.0619; t3 Day100 +0.1023; t4 Day107 +0.1078; t5 Day114 +0.0840; t6_1 started Day91 cost $1.0M, completes Day140, excellent roll +0.5559. Consider t7 ($1.167M) Day98 only if cash after week >~$3.8M and S2 unit economics/capacity remain strong; otherwise wait for liquidity.
 
 ## Enterprise
-- ALWAYS manually query latest open enterprise turns before advancing; reply to every latest non-agent open thread. Use true seats via MAX(seat_count) because agent rows often show seat_count=1.
-- Day84 before actions had 65 latest non-agent open rows: 60 new system E3 leads / 71,872 seats, 4 customer counters, 1 churn-prevention for active #14459. Replied to all; latest non-agent awaiting count should be 0.
-- Current open new-lead E3 pipeline after Day84 actions: 156 leads / 183,708 seats (latest all agent). Offer classes: 66 C49/B39 leads (77,799 seats), 44 old C69/B49 (49,429), 39 C59/B45 (47,656), 4 B29/C49 (4,981), 1 B31.46/C49 (1,735), 1 C49/B45 (1,351), 1 B27.67 (757). New standard offer is C49/B39 to improve conversion under macro/pricing pressure while keeping likely margin (actual E3 usage has been much lower than original estimate).
-- Active E3: #5282 1,152 seats C69, #5955 537 seats C69, #4991 338 seats C42.02, #14459 1,818 seats C69. All 4 now have latest churn-prevention/retention offers from us (C49/B39 for #14459; older retention offers for the first 3). If they reply/counter, retain if price is plausibly above compute: B >=~29 is usually acceptable; C floor now around $49 except strategic retention at C42 can be tolerated.
-- Day84 counters answered: #11408 C$28.31 -> B29/C49; #11410 C$4.87 -> B29/C49; #14458 B$31.46 -> B31.46/C49; #18228 B$13.33 -> B29/C49. Avoid C below ~$42-$49; B around $29-$32 is acceptable given actual E3 usage.
+- ALWAYS manually query latest open enterprise turns before advancing; reply to every latest non-agent open thread (including `system` new leads). Use true seats via MAX(seat_count); agent rows often show seat_count=1.
+- Day91 before actions had 128 latest non-agent open threads: 123 new system E3 leads / 133,874 seats + 5 low-B counters. Replied to all; latest_non_agent_count verified 0.
+- New recession-aware E3 standard offer is B$29/C$49 (month-to-month). Reason: macro PMI 46.1 and counters were low; B29 is near estimated compute breakeven under E3 usage estimate but likely profitable on actual observed usage. #8685 counter B$33.38 accepted (sent B33.38/C49). Low counters #54892 B12.20, #20373 B3.90, #20375 B19.29, #15998 B8.42 were countered with B29/C49.
+- Open E3 pipeline after actions: 279 leads / ~317.6k seats (latest all agent). Classes include 131 B29/C49 leads / 144.9k seats, 66 B39/C49 / 77.8k, 40 old B49/C69 / 44.7k, 38 B45/C59 / 45.9k, plus small leftovers. No new seats accepted during week13, but E3 avg response is slow (~24 days); monitor for acceptances/counters.
+- Active E3: #4991 338 seats C42.02, #5282 1,152 seats C69, #5955 537 seats C69, #14459 1,818 seats C69. All still have latest churn-prevention/retention offers from us (latest agent). Retain where feasible; B >=~29 or C >=~42 is usually acceptable.
+
+## New market discoveries
+- Day91 market research: discovered D_E02 Educational Institutions, D_S07 Data Analysts, D_S10 Music Producers; 16 hidden segments remain.
+- D_S07 (lvl1 noisy): WTP ~$94.5, usage ~180/day, q_min ~0.349, cap ~54k, growth 17.8%; strong network/reputation links with S2/S3. Added D_S07 $30 lead promo and $5k/day targeted dev. Level2 research started, due Day94. Do not buy D_S07 ads until level2 results and organic/network conversion are checked, but it is the best next self-serve expansion candidate as S2 saturates.
+- D_E02 (lvl1 noisy): Educational Institutions, WTP ~$81.8, q_min ~0.304, cap ~183 enterprise accounts, seats 52-735, avg response 7.4d. Level2 research due Day94. Potential enterprise pipeline candidate after info improves.
+- D_S10 (lvl1): WTP ~$99.5 but q_min ~0.63 and high lock-in aversion; likely needs more quality/dev before advertising. Wait.
+- D_E09 Real Estate remains a dud: previous $21k ad test generated zero leads; no spend.
 
 ## Segment/unit-economics notes
-- S2 is still the main cash engine. Info estimate: WTP ~$179/mo, q_min ~0.37, market cap ~167k, annual cap growth ~15%. Active S2 now 75k, so room remains but saturation will matter as active approaches 140k+. Delivered S2 Day84 before R&D completions: A 1.079, B 1.295, C 1.439; huge quality cushion supports the Day84 price lift.
-- Current S2 usage/cost: plan A ~30-60 units/day (~$0.06-$0.12/day compute), plan B ~107-200 units/day (~$0.64-$1.20/day), C tiny volume. No one near quota, but social posts complained about quota/usage ceilings, so quotas were raised and support messaging posted.
-- Watch whether new A quota (5k) cannibalizes B mix. With new prices and $20 lead promo, first cash is A=$49/B=$119/C=$279; recurring A=$69/B=$139/C=$299. Conversion can fall meaningfully and still beat old economics, but if S2 paid conversion drops below ~60% or paid lpd below ~0.045, reconsider price/ad scale.
-- D_E09 Real Estate Groups: level 2 estimates WTP ~$90, q_min 0.425, cap 89, seats 23-332, incoming network from E3/S3. Test ads produced zero leads; all D_E09 spend cut. Do not resume unless organic/network interest appears or more research reveals a channel.
-- Competitor social Day81-84 had several small quality boosts (~0.031-0.036 and 0.032). Existing targeted dev + imminent R&D completions are intended to stay ahead; do not cut S2/E3 dev while price increase is being tested.
+- S2 remains the cash engine. Active S2 115,654 vs noisy cap estimate ~167k; saturation will matter soon. Delivered S2 Day91 before future R&D: A 1.280, B 1.536, C 1.707 with group bonus +1.275, so big quality cushion supports higher new list prices.
+- Current S2 usage: A/B average ~162-166 units/day, max <850 despite A quota 5,000; no one is actually near quotas. Quota social complaints likely lag from old experience/support, not observed cap hits. Keep watching issue age and sentiment.
+- Search ads are lower efficiency (~0.022 leads/$) but still profitable; content remains best (~0.086 leads/$ weekly, variable from 0.048-0.120 daily). Keep $85k/day while paid lpd >~0.05 and conversion >~60%; trim to $55k-$65k if cap saturation collapses leads or issues worsen.
+- Day91 price/promo test: A79/B159 with $30 S2 lead promo keeps A first price unchanged ($49) and raises B first cash only +$10 (to $129), while recurring list increases materially. Watch new S2 conversion, plan mix, and 30-day renewal risk.
 
-## Day 91 priorities
-1. Run `report.py`, `analyze_week.py`, `pipeline_summary.py`, latest-enterprise query, research_projects query, and social posts. Reply to every latest non-agent enterprise thread before advancing.
-2. Evaluate Day84 S2 changes: content/search/social leads per dollar, conversion after price increase, plan mix (especially B share), first-payment cash, and issue age/social sentiment. Keep $85k/day if paid lpd >~0.06 and conversion >~60%; trim to $55k-$65k or restore lower prices if conversion/lpd collapses. If results are excellent and tier5 headroom remains, hold or scale cautiously.
-3. Watch capacity: tier5 is fine unless peak usage >20M/25M or multiple large E3 acceptances arrive. Upgrade to tier6 only if peak >20M, outage risk rises, or enterprise seats surge.
-4. Watch support: S2 open issues can be large but avg age should remain <0.5d. If support/social worsens, raise S2 ops or slow ads.
-5. R&D/liquidity: t1 completes Day86 and t2 Day93. Start tier6 on Day91 only if cash cushion after the week is >~$2.5M and S2 unit economics remain strong; otherwise wait. Consider new market discovery only after cash >~$3M.
-6. Enterprise: new C49/B39 pricing is a conversion test. Track acceptances/counters and actual E3 compute. Retain active E3 accounts where feasible; an accepted B29/B31 deal is usually preferable to losing the account.

    ev=[]

            if random.random()<p:

                ev_day=d+delay
                if ev_day<=DAY+days:
                    plan='C' if random.random()<0.55 else 'B'; price=49.0 if plan=='C' else 29.0
                    ent_events.append({'day':ev_day,'seats':seats,'plan':plan,'price':price,'group':'E3','mod':ev_day%30})
        # Enterprise acceptance events
        for ev in list(ent_events):
            if ev['day']==d:
                cash += ev['seats']*ev['price']
                ents.append({'cid':None,'group':ev['group'],'seats':ev['seats'],'plan':ev['plan'],'price':ev['price'],'mod':d%30,'age':0,'at_risk':False})
                ent_events.remove(ev)
        # churn events
        for e in ents:
            if e.get('churn_day')==d: e['seats']=0
        ents=[e for e in ents if e['seats']>0]
        # Renew small subs
        for c in cohorts:
            if c['age']>0 and c['mod']==d%30 and c['n']>0:
                ret=monthly_ret
                if c['group']!='S2': ret=0.72
                if c['price']>=79: ret-=0.010
                elif c['price']>=69: ret-=0.004
                for bd,b in boosts:
                    if d>=bd: ret+=min(0.018,b*0.08)
                if active>0.88*cap: ret-=0.012
                ret=max(0.80,min(0.995,random.gauss(ret,0.018)))
                c['n']*=ret
                cash += c['n']*c['price']
            c['age']+=1
        # Enterprise renewals
        for e in ents:
            if e['age']>0 and e['mod']==d%30 and e['seats']>0:
                eret=0.88 if e['price']<=49 else 0.80
                for bd,b in boosts:
                    if d>=bd: eret+=min(0.05,b*0.15)
                eret=max(0.55,min(0.98,random.gauss(eret,0.08)))
                if random.random()<eret:
                    cash += e['seats']*e['price']
                else:
                    e['seats']=0
            e['age']+=1
        ents=[e for e in ents if e['seats']>0]
        # costs
        ops=5000+46500; dev=4000+28000; capcost=28000
        cash -= (ad+ops+dev+capcost)
        cash -= leads*0.5
        # compute
        for c in cohorts:
            if c['n']<=0: continue
            if c['plan']=='A': per=max(0.04,random.gauss(0.11,0.035))
            elif c['plan']=='B': per=max(0.35,random.gauss(0.90,0.22))
            else: per=max(0.75,random.gauss(1.9,0.55))
            cash -= c['n']*per
        for e in ents:
            usage=max(25,random.gauss(70,35))
            cost_per=0.006 if e['plan']=='B' else 0.012
            cash -= e['seats']*usage*cost_per
        # occasional outage/reputation/competitor effects
        if random.random()<0.008:
            cash-=random.uniform(50000,300000)
        # planned future R&D if liquidity permits; this is our likely policy.
        for rd_day,cost,name in future_rd:
            if d==rd_day and name not in rd_done and cash>cost+1800000:
                cash-=cost; rd_done.add(name)
    return cash, sum(c['n'] for c in cohorts if c['group']=='S2'), sum(c['n']*c['price'] for c in cohorts), sum(e['seats'] for e in ents)

for days in [7,28,84,182]:
    N=500 if days<=84 else 350
    vals=[]; subs=[]; mrr=[]; seats=[]
    for i in range(N):
        v,s,m,e=simulate(days, days*100000+i)
        vals.append(v); subs.append(s); mrr.append(m); seats.append(e)
    print(days, 'mean',round(statistics.mean(vals)), 'median',round(statistics.median(vals)), 'p025',round(pct(vals,0.025)), 'p10',round(pct(vals,0.10)), 'p90',round(pct(vals,0.90)), 'p975',round(pct(vals,0.975)), 'subs_med',round(statistics.median(subs)), 'mrr_med',round(statistics.median(mrr)), 'ent_seats_med',round(statistics.median(seats)))

    print(f"\n=== {name} ===")

        return r
    except Exception as e:

WITH last AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) AS mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id),
mx AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(seat_count) AS seats, MIN(day) AS first_day FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT t.customer_id, c.group_id, t.sender, t.thread_type, t.turn_number, t.day, mx.seats, t.offer_json, t.message_text
FROM last l JOIN enterprise_turns t ON t.message_id=l.mid
JOIN mx ON mx.customer_id=t.customer_id
JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=t.customer_id
WHERE t.closed=0 AND t.sender <> 'agent'
ORDER BY t.day, t.customer_id
''')['rows']
